subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Great Debate of 1920
description: A fast breakdown of The Great Debate of 1920 for builders.

hook.text: In 1920, two astronomers publicly debated whether the Milky Way was the entire universe or just one of many galaxies.

buildup.text: Harlow Shapley argued the Milky Way was everything. Heber Curtis said 'spiral nebulae' were separate galaxies far away.

discovery.text: Edwin Hubble settled it in 1924 by measuring the distance to Andromeda—far beyond the Milky Way's edge.

twist.text: The universe suddenly became billions of times larger. Our galaxy was just one island in a vast cosmic ocean.

climax.text: Today we know there are at least 2 trillion galaxies. Shapley's Milky Way was a speck in an ocean he couldn't imagine.

punchline.text: One debate, one measurement. The universe grew a billion times overnight.

quiz.question: What did Hubble's 1924 measurement of Andromeda's distance prove?
options: The Milky Way is just one of many galaxies in the universe
The universe is shrinking over time
All spiral nebulae are part of the Milky Way
